compiled_state.title: sprint-list-epics --has-tag filter and tags in index builder
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
As a developer of sprint's epic-selection flow, I want a reliable tag-based filter so that consumers can query for epics carrying specific tags without inferring via child counts.

## Done Definition
- sprint-list-epics.sh argument parser accepts `--has-tag=<tag>` (net-new flag, does not currently exist)
- `_build_index()` Python block includes each ticket's `tags` field (list of strings) in the emitted entry dict
- Filter block reads `tags` from the entry and excludes tickets whose tag list does not contain the requested tag
- Fixture test: 3 tickets (2 tagged with target, 1 without) — filter returns exactly the 2 tagged tickets
- Existing flags (--min-children, --max-children, --all) continue to work unchanged (backward-compat)

## Considerations
- [Testing] Update tests/scripts/test-sprint-list-epics.sh with --has-tag cases including positive match, negative match, and empty-tags cases

## Escalation Policy
Escalate when blocked — agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required that could misdirect implementation. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference.

comments.body: Fixed: --has-tag=<tag> flag added to sprint-list-epics.sh; _build_index() emits tags field; filter excludes tickets without requested tag. Tests 45-47 added to test-sprint-list-epics.sh (positive/negative/empty-tags) — all 47 pass. .test-index mapping added. Verifier PASS on all 5 done definitions.
comments.author: Test
